title: How Much Space Does a Bouncy Castle Need? | Nova Kingdom Rentals
metaTitle: How Much Space Does a Bouncy Castle Need? | Nova Kingdom Rentals
metaDescription: Learn how much space a bouncy castle needs for your backyard or event. Setup requirements, clearance rules, and tips from Nova Kingdom Rentals, Bridgewater NS.
h1: How Much Space Does a Bouncy Castle Need?
intro: One of the most common questions before renting a bouncy castle is whether it will fit. This guide covers the setup space requirements for Nova Kingdom Rentals inflatables, including clearance, surface types, and what to measure before you book. We are based in Bridgewater, Nova Scotia, and our setup crew confirms suitability before every booking.
serviceAreaText: Nova Kingdom Rentals is based in Bridgewater, NS and serves the South Shore and Nova Scotia.
bestFor: Anyone comparing inflatable sizes before booking
Event coordinators confirming setup space at a venue
School or community event planners checking available space

faq.question: How much space does a bouncy castle need?
faq.answer: Each inflatable has different space requirements. As a general rule, add 2–3 feet of clearance on all sides of the unit dimensions, plus overhead clearance (most inflatables are 10–15 feet tall). The Crown Dino Combo, for example, is 13.1ft x 13.1ft x 9.8ft and needs a clear area of at least 18ft x 18ft with 12ft overhead clearance.

faq.question: What surface types work for inflatable setup?
faq.answer: Flat grass is the ideal setup surface. We can also set up on concrete, asphalt, and compacted gravel — stakes may not be used on hard surfaces, so sandbags or ballast weights are used instead. Sloped surfaces and soft sand are generally not suitable. Surface suitability is confirmed before booking.

faq.question: Do I need to measure my yard before booking?
faq.answer: Yes. We recommend measuring the available flat area before submitting a booking request. Include the length, width, and any overhead obstacles like trees, power lines, or deck covers. We confirm setup suitability before finalising the booking.

faq.question: What is the overhead clearance required?
faq.answer: Most inflatables require 12–15 feet of overhead clearance. The Crown Rush 42 water slide requires up to 12 feet at its highest point. Always account for trees, wires, and canopy structures when measuring available height.

ctaHeading: Ready to book? Check space requirements for your rental
ctaText: Tell us your event date, address, and setup space dimensions. We will confirm which inflatables fit and handle everything from delivery to takedown.

title: Wet vs Dry Inflatables — Which Should You Rent? | Nova Kingdom Rentals
metaTitle: Wet vs Dry Inflatables — Which Should You Rent? | Nova Kingdom Rentals
metaDescription: Wet vs dry inflatables — which is right for your event? Compare options, setup needs, and pricing from Nova Kingdom Rentals in Bridgewater, Nova Scotia.
h1: Wet vs Dry Inflatables: Which Is Right for Your Event?
intro: Not every inflatable is water-ready, and not every event calls for a water slide. This guide explains the difference between wet and dry inflatables, which Nova Kingdom Rentals products fall into each category, and how to choose based on your event, guests, and setup. We are Bridgewater-based and serve the South Shore and Nova Scotia.
serviceAreaText: Nova Kingdom Rentals is based in Bridgewater, NS and serves the South Shore, Nova Scotia, and selected events across the Maritimes.
bestFor: Summer birthday parties choosing between wet and dry fun
Parents comparing options for younger vs older kids
Event planners booking for venues without water access
Anyone deciding between inflatables for hot-weather events

faq.question: What is the difference between a wet and dry inflatable?
faq.answer: Wet inflatables are designed to run with water — they include water slides, splash pools, and combo units with wet zones. Dry inflatables are used without water and include bounce houses, obstacle courses, small climbing inflatables, and interactive games. Nova Kingdom Rentals wet-use units are the Crown Island Combo, Crown Rush 42, and Crown Cascade.

faq.question: Do wet inflatables require a water connection?
faq.answer: Yes. Wet inflatables require access to a garden hose and water supply at the setup location. The hose attaches to a spray bar or soaker inlet on the unit. You are responsible for water access at your location.

faq.question: Can dry inflatables be used in hot weather?
faq.answer: Yes. Dry inflatables can be used in all weather conditions within our operating guidelines. In hot weather, dry bounce inflatables are still popular — the Crown Dino Combo, Crown Quest obstacle course, Crown Climber (great for younger kids), and Crown Axe Challenge are all dry units suitable for warm-weather events.

faq.question: Which wet inflatable is best for young kids?
faq.answer: The Crown Island Combo is the best wet inflatable for younger kids. It has a splash pool, slide, and bounce area, with a seat height of 4ft 3in — accessible for children 3 and up. The Crown Cascade and Crown Rush 42 are better suited to older kids and teens.

ctaHeading: Not sure which inflatable fits your event?
ctaText: Send your event details — date, address, number of guests, and age range. We will recommend the right inflatable or package and confirm availability.

faq.question: Do I need to supply water?
faq.answer: Yes. You must provide a water source, hose access, and a safe drainage area. Water should be turned off at least 1 hour before scheduled pickup.

faq.question: What surface is needed for a water slide?

faq.question: How old do kids need to be to use a water slide?
